description: The main criminal code of India covering offences and penalties

sections.title: Acts done by several persons in furtherance of common intention
sections.chapter: II - General Explanations
sections.text: When a criminal act is done by several persons in furtherance of the common intention of all, each of such persons is liable for that act in the same manner as if it were done by him alone.
sections.simplified: If multiple people commit a crime together with a shared plan, each person is equally responsible as if they did it alone.

sections.title: Things done in private defence
sections.chapter: IV - Right of Private Defence
sections.text: Nothing is an offence which is done in the exercise of the right of private defence.
sections.simplified: If you do something to protect yourself or your property from an attack, it is not considered a crime. This is called the right of self-defense.

sections.title: Right of private defence of the body and of property
sections.chapter: IV - Right of Private Defence
sections.text: Every person has a right, subject to the restrictions contained in section 99, to defend his own body, and the body of any other person, against any offence affecting the human body. The same right extends to protecting movable or immovable property against theft, robbery, mischief or criminal trespass.
sections.simplified: Every person has the right to defend their body and another person's body from attacks. You can also defend your property from theft, robbery, damage, or trespass.

sections.title: When the right of private defence of the body extends to causing death
sections.chapter: IV - Right of Private Defence
sections.text: The right of private defence of the body extends, under the restrictions mentioned in the last preceding section, to the voluntary causing of death or of any other harm to the assailant, if the offence which occasions the exercise of the right be of any of the descriptions hereinafter enumerated: assault with reasonable apprehension of death, assault with intention to commit rape, assault with intention to gratify unnatural lust, assault with intention to kidnap or abduct, assault with intention to wrongfully confine a person under circumstances which may reasonably cause apprehension of inability to have recourse to the public authorities.
sections.simplified: You have the right to kill an attacker in self-defense if they are trying to: kill you, rape you, kidnap you, or confine you and you cannot get help from authorities.

sections.title: Definition of criminal conspiracy
sections.chapter: VA - Criminal Conspiracy
sections.text: When two or more persons agree to do, or cause to be done, an illegal act, or an act which is not illegal by illegal means, such an agreement is designated a criminal conspiracy.
sections.simplified: Criminal conspiracy means when two or more people plan together to do something illegal or to do something legal through illegal methods.

sections.title: Punishment of criminal conspiracy
sections.chapter: VA - Criminal Conspiracy
sections.text: Whoever is a party to a criminal conspiracy to commit an offence punishable with death, imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment for a term of two years or upwards, shall be punished in the same manner as if he had abetted such offence.
sections.simplified: If you participate in a criminal conspiracy for a serious crime, you will be punished as if you actually helped commit that crime.
sections.punishment: Same as abettor of the offence

sections.title: Unlawful assembly
sections.chapter: VIII - Of Offences Against the Public Tranquility
sections.text: An assembly of five or more persons is designated an unlawful assembly if the common object of the persons composing that assembly is to commit any offence, or to resist the execution of any law, or to enforce any right by criminal force.
sections.simplified: A group of 5 or more persons becomes an unlawful assembly if they come together to commit a crime, resist law enforcement, or use force to claim a right.

sections.title: Every member of unlawful assembly guilty of offence committed in prosecution of common object
sections.chapter: VIII - Of Offences Against the Public Tranquility
sections.text: If an offence is committed by any member of an unlawful assembly in prosecution of the common object of that assembly, or such as the members of that assembly knew to be likely to be committed in prosecution of that object, every person who at the time of the committing of that offence is a member of the same assembly is guilty of that offence.
sections.simplified: If any member of an unlawful assembly commits a crime as part of the group's shared purpose, every member of that group is considered guilty of that crime.

sections.title: Promoting enmity between different groups on grounds of religion, race etc.
sections.chapter: VIII - Of Offences Against the Public Tranquility
sections.text: Whoever by words, either spoken or written, or by signs or by visible representations or otherwise, promotes or attempts to promote, on grounds of religion, race, place of birth, residence, language, caste or community or any other ground whatsoever, disharmony or feelings of enmity, hatred or ill-will between different religious, racial, language or regional groups or castes or communities, shall be punished with imprisonment which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
sections.simplified: Anyone who tries to create hatred or conflict between different religious, racial, caste, or language groups through speech, writing, or actions can be jailed up to 3 years and fined.
sections.punishment: Up to 3 years imprisonment and/or fine

sections.title: Public servant disobeying direction under law
sections.chapter: IX - Of Offences by or Relating to Public Servants
sections.text: Whoever, being a public servant, knowingly disobeys any direction of the law which prohibits him from requiring the attendance at any place of any person for the purpose of investigation into an offence or any other matter, or knowingly disobeys, to the prejudice of any person, any other direction of the law regulating the manner in which he shall conduct such investigation, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than six months but which may extend to two years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.simplified: If a police officer or government official refuses to file your FIR or follow proper investigation procedures, they can be punished with 6 months to 2 years in jail and a fine.
sections.punishment: 6 months to 2 years imprisonment and fine

sections.title: Public nuisance
sections.chapter: XIV - Of Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience
sections.text: A person is guilty of a public nuisance who does any act or is guilty of an illegal omission which causes any common injury, danger or annoyance to the public or to the people in general who dwell or occupy property in the vicinity.
sections.simplified: A person commits public nuisance when they do something (or fail to do something) that causes harm, danger, or annoyance to the general public or people living nearby.

sections.title: Deliberate and malicious acts intended to outrage religious feelings
sections.chapter: XV - Of Offences Relating to Religion
sections.text: Whoever, with deliberate and malicious intention of outraging the religious feelings of any class of citizens of India, by words, either spoken or written, or by signs or by visible representations or otherwise, insults or attempts to insult the religion or the religious beliefs of that class, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
sections.simplified: Anyone who deliberately and maliciously insults or attempts to insult the religion of any group of citizens can be imprisoned up to 3 years and/or fined.
sections.punishment: Up to 3 years imprisonment and/or fine

sections.title: Culpable homicide
sections.chapter: XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Whoever causes death by doing an act with the intention of causing death, or with the intention of causing such bodily injury as is likely to cause death, or with the knowledge that he is likely by such act to cause death, commits the offence of culpable homicide.
sections.simplified: Culpable homicide is when someone causes another person's death by intentionally doing an act that they know could cause death. It is different from murder (Section 300) based on the degree of intention.

sections.title: Murder
sections.chapter: XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Culpable homicide is murder if the act by which the death is caused is done with the intention of causing death, or if it is done with the intention of causing such bodily injury as the offender knows to be likely to cause the death of the person, or if it is done with the intention of causing bodily injury to any person and the bodily injury intended to be inflicted is sufficient in the ordinary course of nature to cause death.
sections.simplified: Murder is when someone intentionally kills another person, or causes injuries knowing those injuries will likely cause death. The key difference from culpable homicide is the higher degree of intention.

sections.title: Punishment for murder
sections.chapter: XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Whoever commits murder shall be punished with death, or imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.simplified: If someone is found guilty of murder, they can be sentenced to death or life imprisonment, and may also have to pay a fine.
sections.punishment: Death or life imprisonment and fine

sections.title: Punishment for culpable homicide not amounting to murder
sections.chapter: XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Whoever commits culpable homicide not amounting to murder shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.simplified: If someone kills another person but it doesn't qualify as murder (less intention), they can be imprisoned for life or up to 10 years and fined.
sections.punishment: Life imprisonment or up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Causing death by negligence
sections.chapter: XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Whoever causes the death of any person by doing any rash or negligent act not amounting to culpable homicide, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
sections.simplified: If someone causes another person's death through carelessness or negligence (like reckless driving), they can be jailed up to 2 years and/or fined.
sections.punishment: Up to 2 years imprisonment and/or fine

sections.title: Dowry death
sections.chapter: XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Where the death of a woman is caused by any burns or bodily injury or occurs otherwise than under normal circumstances within seven years of her marriage and it is shown that soon before her death she was subjected to cruelty or harassment by her husband or any relative of her husband for, or in connection with, any demand for dowry, such death shall be called dowry death.
sections.simplified: If a woman dies within 7 years of marriage due to burns, injuries, or unnatural causes, and she was harassed for dowry before death, it is treated as 'dowry death'. The husband and his relatives are presumed guilty.
sections.punishment: Minimum 7 years, up to life imprisonment

sections.title: Abetment of suicide
sections.chapter: XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: If any person commits suicide, whoever abets the commission of such suicide, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.simplified: If someone drives another person to commit suicide through instigation, conspiracy, or aid, they can be punished with up to 10 years in jail and a fine.
sections.punishment: Up to 10 years imprisonment and fine

sections.title: Attempt to murder
sections.chapter: XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Whoever does any act with such intention or knowledge, and under such circumstances that, if he by that act caused death, he would be guilty of murder, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.simplified: If someone tries to murder another person but fails, they can be imprisoned up to 10 years. If the victim is hurt, the punishment can extend to life imprisonment.
sections.punishment: Up to 10 years or life imprisonment with fine

sections.title: Hurt
sections.chapter: XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Whoever causes bodily pain, disease or infirmity to any person is said to cause hurt.
sections.simplified: Causing physical pain, disease, or weakness to another person is legally called 'hurt'.

sections.title: Grievous hurt
sections.chapter: XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: The following kinds of hurt only are designated as grievous: emasculation, permanent privation of the sight of either eye, permanent privation of the hearing of either ear, privation of any member or joint, destruction or permanent impairing of the powers of any member or joint, permanent disfiguration of the head or face, fracture or dislocation of a bone or tooth, any hurt which endangers life or which causes the sufferer to be during the space of twenty days in severe bodily pain.
sections.simplified: Grievous hurt includes: loss of an eye or ear, loss of a limb, permanent disfigurement, bone fracture, or any injury that endangers life or causes severe pain for 20+ days.

sections.title: Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt
sections.chapter: XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Whoever, except in the case provided for by section 334, voluntarily causes hurt, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.
sections.simplified: If you intentionally hurt someone physically, you can be jailed up to 1 year and/or fined up to Rs 1,000.
sections.punishment: Up to 1 year imprisonment and/or fine up to Rs 1,000

sections.title: Punishment for voluntarily causing grievous hurt
sections.chapter: XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Whoever, except in the case provided for by section 335, voluntarily causes grievous hurt, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.simplified: If you intentionally cause serious injuries (like fractures, loss of limb, or disfigurement), you can be jailed up to 7 years and fined.
sections.punishment: Up to 7 years imprisonment and fine

sections.title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt by dangerous weapons or means
sections.chapter: XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Whoever, except in the case provided for by section 335, voluntarily causes grievous hurt by means of any instrument for shooting, stabbing or cutting, or any instrument which, used as a weapon of offence, is likely to cause death, or by means of fire or any heated substance, or by means of any poison or any corrosive substance, or by means of any explosive substance, or by means of any substance which it is deleterious to the human body to inhale, to swallow, or to receive into the blood, or by means of any animal, shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.simplified: If someone causes serious injuries using weapons, fire, acid, poison, or dangerous substances, they face life imprisonment or up to 10 years in jail and a fine.
sections.punishment: Life imprisonment or up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt by use of acid, etc.
sections.chapter: XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Whoever causes permanent or partial damage or deformity to, or burns or maims or disfigures or disables, any part or parts of the body of a person or causes grievous hurt by throwing acid on or by administering acid to that person, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than ten years but which may extend to imprisonment for life, and with fine.
sections.simplified: Acid attacks are punishable with a minimum of 10 years in prison, which can extend to life imprisonment, along with a fine. The fine is to be paid to the victim for medical expenses.
sections.punishment: Minimum 10 years, up to life imprisonment and fine

sections.title: Wrongful restraint
sections.chapter: XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Whoever voluntarily obstructs any person so as to prevent that person from proceeding in any direction in which that person has a right to proceed, is said wrongfully to restrain that person.
sections.simplified: Wrongful restraint means blocking someone from moving freely in a direction they have the right to go.

sections.title: Wrongful confinement
sections.chapter: XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Whoever wrongfully restrains any person in such a manner as to prevent that person from proceeding beyond certain circumscribing limits, is said wrongfully to confine that person.
sections.simplified: Wrongful confinement means locking someone up or preventing them from leaving a certain area. This is a more severe form of wrongful restraint.

sections.title: Assault or criminal force to woman with intent to outrage her modesty
sections.chapter: XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any woman, intending to outrage or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby outrage her modesty, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than one year but which may extend to five years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.simplified: If someone physically assaults a woman or uses force against her with the intention of outraging her modesty, they face 1 to 5 years in prison and a fine.
sections.punishment: 1 to 5 years imprisonment and fine

sections.title: Sexual harassment and punishment for sexual harassment
sections.chapter: XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: A man committing any of the following acts: physical contact and advances involving unwelcome and explicit sexual overtures, a demand or request for sexual favours, showing pornography against the will of a woman, making sexually coloured remarks, shall be guilty of the offence of sexual harassment.
sections.simplified: Sexual harassment includes: unwanted physical contact, demanding sexual favours, showing porn to a woman, or making sexual remarks. Punishment is up to 3 years imprisonment and/or fine.
sections.punishment: Up to 3 years imprisonment and/or fine

sections.title: Stalking
sections.chapter: XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Any man who follows a woman and contacts, or attempts to contact such woman to foster personal interaction repeatedly despite a clear indication of disinterest by such woman, or monitors the use by a woman of the internet, email or any other form of electronic communication, commits the offence of stalking.
sections.simplified: Stalking means repeatedly following a woman, contacting her despite rejection, or monitoring her online activity. First offence: up to 3 years. Second offence: up to 5 years.
sections.punishment: First offence: up to 3 years. Repeat: up to 5 years

sections.title: Kidnapping
sections.chapter: XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Kidnapping is of two kinds: kidnapping from India, and kidnapping from lawful guardianship.
sections.simplified: Kidnapping means taking someone away from India or taking a minor (under 16 for boys, under 18 for girls) away from their legal guardian without permission.

sections.title: Punishment for kidnapping
sections.chapter: XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Whoever kidnaps any person from India or from lawful guardianship, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.simplified: Kidnapping is punishable with up to 7 years imprisonment and fine.
sections.punishment: Up to 7 years imprisonment and fine

sections.title: Rape
sections.chapter: XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: A man is said to commit rape if he has sexual intercourse with a woman under circumstances falling under any of the following descriptions: against her will, without her consent, with her consent obtained by putting her or any person she is interested in fear of death or hurt, with her consent when the man knows he is not her husband and she has given consent believing him to be her husband, with her consent when she is unable to communicate consent, or with or without her consent when she is under eighteen years of age.
sections.simplified: Rape is defined as sexual intercourse with a woman: against her will, without consent, with consent obtained through fear/threat, when she can't give valid consent, or with a minor (under 18). Marital rape exception exists for wives over 18.

sections.title: Punishment for rape
sections.chapter: XVI - Of Offences Affecting the Human Body
sections.text: Whoever commits rape shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than ten years, but which may extend to imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.simplified: Rape carries a minimum punishment of 10 years rigorous imprisonment, which can extend to life imprisonment, plus a fine.
sections.punishment: Minimum 10 years RI, up to life imprisonment and fine

sections.title: Theft
sections.chapter: XVII - Of Offences Against Property
sections.text: Whoever, intending to take dishonestly any moveable property out of the possession of any person without that person's consent, moves that property in order to such taking, is said to commit theft.
sections.simplified: Theft means dishonestly taking someone's movable property (like phone, money, jewelry) without their permission.

sections.title: Punishment for theft
sections.chapter: XVII - Of Offences Against Property
sections.text: Whoever commits theft shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
sections.simplified: Theft is punishable with up to 3 years in prison and/or a fine.
sections.punishment: Up to 3 years imprisonment and/or fine

sections.title: Theft in dwelling house, etc.
sections.chapter: XVII - Of Offences Against Property
sections.text: Whoever commits theft in any building, tent or vessel, which building, tent or vessel is used as a human dwelling, or used for the custody of property, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.simplified: Theft from someone's home, building, or storage area carries a stricter punishment of up to 7 years in jail and a fine.
sections.punishment: Up to 7 years imprisonment and fine

sections.title: Extortion
sections.chapter: XVII - Of Offences Against Property
sections.text: Whoever intentionally puts any person in fear of any injury to that person, or to any other, and thereby dishonestly induces the person so put in fear to deliver to any person any property or valuable security, or anything signed or sealed which may be converted into a valuable security, commits extortion.
sections.simplified: Extortion means threatening someone to force them to give you money, property, or valuables. It's using fear to take someone's things.

sections.title: Punishment for extortion
sections.chapter: XVII - Of Offences Against Property
sections.text: Whoever commits extortion shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
sections.simplified: Extortion is punishable with up to 3 years imprisonment and/or fine.
sections.punishment: Up to 3 years imprisonment and/or fine

sections.title: Robbery
sections.chapter: XVII - Of Offences Against Property
sections.text: In all robbery there is either theft or extortion. When theft is robbery: Theft is robbery if, in order to the committing of the theft, or in committing the theft, or in carrying away or attempting to carry away property obtained by the theft, the offender causes death, hurt or wrongful restraint, or fear of instant death, instant hurt or instant wrongful restraint.
sections.simplified: Robbery is theft combined with violence or threats. If someone steals while threatening, hurting, or frightening you, it becomes robbery.

sections.title: Punishment for robbery
sections.chapter: XVII - Of Offences Against Property
sections.text: Whoever commits robbery shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.simplified: Robbery is punishable with up to 10 years rigorous imprisonment and fine. If committed on a highway between sunset and sunrise, the punishment can extend to 14 years.
sections.punishment: Up to 10 years RI and fine

sections.title: Criminal breach of trust
sections.chapter: XVII - Of Offences Against Property
sections.text: Whoever, being in any manner entrusted with property, or with any dominion over property, dishonestly misappropriates or converts to his own use that property, or dishonestly uses or disposes of that property, commits criminal breach of trust.
sections.simplified: If someone is trusted with property or money and misuses it for their own benefit, it's called criminal breach of trust. Example: an employee stealing from the company.

sections.title: Punishment for criminal breach of trust
sections.chapter: XVII - Of Offences Against Property
sections.text: Whoever commits criminal breach of trust shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
sections.simplified: Criminal breach of trust is punishable with up to 3 years in prison and/or a fine.
sections.punishment: Up to 3 years imprisonment and/or fine

sections.title: Cheating
sections.chapter: XVII - Of Offences Against Property
sections.text: Whoever, by deceiving any person, fraudulently or dishonestly induces the person so deceived to deliver any property to any person, or to consent that any person shall retain any property, or intentionally induces the person so deceived to do or omit to do anything which he would not do or omit if he were not so deceived, and which act or omission causes or is likely to cause damage or harm to that person in body, mind, reputation or property, is said to cheat.
sections.simplified: Cheating means deceiving someone to get them to give you property, money, or do something harmful to themselves. Examples: fake products, fraudulent schemes, false promises.

sections.title: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property
sections.chapter: XVII - Of Offences Against Property
sections.text: Whoever cheats and thereby dishonestly induces the person deceived to deliver any property to any person, or to make, alter or destroy the whole or any part of a valuable security, or anything which is signed or sealed, and which is capable of being converted into a valuable security, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.simplified: If someone cheats you and makes you hand over property, money, or sign documents, they can be jailed up to 7 years and fined. This is the famous 'Section 420' used for serious fraud cases.
sections.punishment: Up to 7 years imprisonment and fine

sections.title: Forgery
sections.chapter: XVIII - Of Offences Relating to Documents
sections.text: Whoever makes any false document or false electronic record or part of a document or electronic record, with intent to cause damage or injury, to the public or to any person, or to support any claim or title, or to cause any person to part with property, or to enter into any express or implied contract, or with intent to commit fraud or that fraud may be committed, commits forgery.
sections.simplified: Forgery means creating a fake document or electronic record to cheat someone, claim property, or commit fraud.

sections.title: Forgery for purpose of cheating
sections.chapter: XVIII - Of Offences Relating to Documents
sections.text: Whoever commits forgery, intending that the document or electronic record forged shall be used for the purpose of cheating, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.simplified: Creating a fake document specifically to cheat someone is punishable with up to 7 years in jail and a fine.
sections.punishment: Up to 7 years imprisonment and fine

sections.title: Marrying again during lifetime of husband or wife
sections.chapter: XX - Of Offences Relating to Marriage
sections.text: Whoever, having a husband or wife living, marries in any case in which such marriage is void by reason of its taking place during the life of such husband or wife, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.simplified: Bigamy (marrying another person while your first spouse is alive) is punishable with up to 7 years imprisonment and fine.
sections.punishment: Up to 7 years imprisonment and fine

sections.title: Adultery (Struck Down)
sections.chapter: XX - Of Offences Relating to Marriage
sections.text: Section 497 was struck down by the Supreme Court of India in Joseph Shine v. Union of India (2018) as unconstitutional. Adultery is no longer a criminal offence in India.
sections.simplified: Adultery is no longer a crime in India. The Supreme Court struck down this section in 2018, declaring it unconstitutional. However, adultery can still be a ground for divorce.

sections.title: Husband or relative of husband of a woman subjecting her to cruelty
sections.chapter: XXa - Of Cruelty by Husband or Relatives of Husband
sections.text: Whoever, being the husband or the relative of the husband of a woman, subjects such woman to cruelty shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to three years and shall also be liable to fine. Cruelty means any willful conduct which is of such a nature as is likely to drive the woman to commit suicide or to cause grave injury or danger to life, limb or health, or harassment of the woman where such harassment is with a view to coercing her or any person related to her to meet any unlawful demand for any property or valuable security or is on account of failure by her or any person related to her to meet such demand.
sections.simplified: If a husband or his relatives torture or harass a woman (mentally or physically), especially for dowry, they can be jailed up to 3 years and fined. This is a non-bailable and cognizable offence.
sections.punishment: Up to 3 years imprisonment and fine (non-bailable)

sections.title: Defamation
sections.chapter: XXI - Of Defamation
sections.text: Whoever, by words either spoken or intended to be read, or by signs or by visible representations, makes or publishes any imputation concerning any person intending to harm, or knowing or having reason to believe that such imputation will harm, the reputation of such person, is said to defame that person.
sections.simplified: Defamation means making or publishing false statements (spoken or written) that damage someone's reputation. There are exceptions for truth told in public interest, fair comments, etc.

sections.title: Punishment for defamation
sections.chapter: XXI - Of Defamation
sections.text: Whoever defames another shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
sections.simplified: Defamation is punishable with up to 2 years imprisonment and/or fine.
sections.punishment: Up to 2 years imprisonment and/or fine

sections.title: Criminal intimidation
sections.chapter: XXII - Of Criminal Intimidation, Insult and Annoyance
sections.text: Whoever threatens another with any injury to his person, reputation or property, or to the person or reputation of any one in whom that person is interested, with intent to cause alarm to that person, or to cause that person to do any act which he is not legally bound to do, or to omit to do any act which that person is legally entitled to do, as the means of avoiding the execution of such threat, commits criminal intimidation.
sections.simplified: Criminal intimidation means threatening someone to scare them into doing something or not doing something. This includes threats to hurt them, damage their reputation, or destroy their property.

sections.title: Punishment for criminal intimidation
sections.chapter: XXII - Of Criminal Intimidation, Insult and Annoyance
sections.text: Whoever commits the offence of criminal intimidation shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both. If threat be to cause death or grievous hurt, etc., shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both.
sections.simplified: Criminal intimidation is punishable with up to 2 years imprisonment. If the threat involves death or serious injury, the punishment can extend to 7 years.
sections.punishment: Up to 2 years (general), up to 7 years (death/grievous hurt threats)

sections.title: Word, gesture or act intended to insult the modesty of a woman
sections.chapter: XXII - Of Criminal Intimidation, Insult and Annoyance
sections.text: Whoever, intending to insult the modesty of any woman, utters any word, makes any sound or gesture, or exhibits any object, intending that such word or sound shall be heard, or that such gesture or object shall be seen, by such woman, or intrudes upon the privacy of such woman, shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to three years, and also with fine.
sections.simplified: Making obscene gestures, words, sounds, or invading a woman's privacy to insult her modesty is punishable with up to 3 years in jail and a fine.
sections.punishment: Up to 3 years imprisonment and fine
